hero.tagline: The essence of chamber music, drawn in strings alone
hero.subtitle: A string chamber ensemble based in Daegu, breathing with the world

home.intro_title: Chamber music shaped by the depth of strings
home.intro_lead: A string chamber ensemble from Daegu, formed by musicians who have earned recognition as soloists and chamber players both at home and abroad. With a solidity and gravity rivaling that of an orchestra, they present the very essence of chamber music.
home.members_lead: Meet the concertmasters and principal players who lead the ensemble.
home.history_lead: A record of our growth into one of Daegu's representative chamber ensembles since our debut in 2020.
home.support_note: Supported by the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism · Selected for the 2026 Regional Representative Arts Organization Support Program

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Daegu Virtuoso Chamber
body: Daegu Virtuoso Chamber is a chamber ensemble formed by musicians who, based in Daegu, have built a profound musical world together. Its members are players who have earned solid recognition as soloists and chamber musicians both at home and abroad.
Drawing delicate melodies with strings alone, the ensemble presents the very essence of chamber music through performances imbued with a solidity and gravity rivaling that of an orchestra.

The ensemble has presented a wide range of programs alongside musicians actively performing both at home and abroad, including conductors Yeo Ja-kyung and Tania Miller, violinists Soyoung Yoon and Dami Kim, flutist Jasmine Choi, and pianists Jinsang Lee and Julius-Jeongwon Kim.

Acclaimed for a diverse repertoire that brings out the charm of chamber music, the ensemble plans to continue its international exchange activities in 2026 through a tour that includes exchange concerts with UNESCO Cities of Music in Germany and France.

Selected for the ' 2026 Regional Representative Arts Organization Support Program' sponsored by the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism, the ensemble continues to strive steadily to promote Daegu as a city of culture and the arts.
about.collab_lead: We have deepened our artistry by sharing the stage with world-class conductors and performers from home and abroad.
about.guest_principal_lead: The cellist who joins us as Guest Principal.

han-gyeongjin.name: Han Gyeong-jin
bio: Graduated from Yewon School
Admitted as a gifted student to the School of Music at Korea National University of Arts and graduated with a Bachelor of Music
Diplom from Berlin University of the Arts (UdK), Germany
Konzertexamen (Soloist Diploma) from the University of Music and Theatre Leipzig, Germany
Meisterklassenexamen from the University of Music and Theatre Leipzig, Germany
Served as Concertmaster of the Suwon Philharmonic Orchestra

Won prizes at numerous competitions including the Shimonoseki International Competition, the Nuri International Competition, the Dong-A Music Competition, the KBS New Artist Music Competition, and the Busan Music Competition

Performed as soloist with numerous orchestras including the MDR Philharmonic Orchestra, the Jena Philharmonic Orchestra, the Istanbul Philharmonic Orchestra, KCO, the Seoul Philharmonic Orchestra, the KBS Symphony Orchestra, the Korean National Symphony Orchestra, the Gangnam Symphony Orchestra, the Daegu Symphony Orchestra, the Busan Philharmonic Orchestra, the Changwon Philharmonic Orchestra, the Suwon Philharmonic Orchestra, the Cheongju Philharmonic Orchestra, the Chuncheon Philharmonic Orchestra, and the Gyeongsan Philharmonic Orchestra
Currently: Professor at Kyungpook National University, Concertmaster of KCO (formerly the Baroque Ensemble), Leader of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, Member of the Kairos Ensemble

kim-namhoon.name: Kim Nam-hoon
bio: Graduated from Yewon School, graduated early from Seoul Arts High School, admitted as a gifted student to Korea National University of Arts, and graduated from both the Bachelor of Music and Soloist programs
Master of Music from the Manhattan School of Music, completed Professional Studies, and Doctor of Musical Arts from Rutgers University (Mason Gross School of the Arts)

1st prize at the Music Association Overseas Dispatch Competition, 1st prize at the Ewha-Kyunghyang Competition, 2nd prize at the JoongAng Ilbo Competition, 1st prize at the Korea Philharmonic Competition, 2nd prize at the Hankook Ilbo Competition, and 2nd prize at the Chosun Ilbo Competition

Performed as soloist with various orchestras at home and abroad including the Seoul Symphony, the Seoul Chamber, the Wonju Philharmonic Orchestra, the Prime Philharmonic Orchestra, the Plymouth Philharmonic Orchestra, the MAV Symphony Orchestra, and the Georgia State University Symphony Orchestra

Homecoming recital at the IBK Chamber Hall of the Seoul Arts Center, invited recitals at the Sejong Center, the Kumho Young Artist series, and the Tongyeong International Music Foundation, the Petit Classical Concert at the Korean Cultural Center of the Korean Embassy in the United States, a recital at Carnegie Weill Hall, and a JSAC-invited Gala Concert
Chamber music activities including the Hwaum Chamber Orchestra, the Busan Cultural Center Chamber Festival, the Seokjojeon Concert, and Trio ON

Currently: Professor in the Department of Orchestral Instruments at the College of Music and Performing Arts, Keimyung University; Member of Trio ON, the Hwaum Chamber Orchestra, Leader of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, and Member of the New Old Ensemble, Ensemble V9, and Ensemble The First

lee-kangwon.name: Lee Kang-won
bio: Graduated from Seoul Arts High School
Bachelor's and Master's degrees from Berlin University of the Arts (UdK)
Graduated with highest honors from the Soloist program at Detmold University of Music
Performed as soloist with the UdK Chamber Orchestra, the Seongnam Philharmonic Orchestra, the Daegu Symphony Orchestra, the Gangnam Symphony Orchestra, and the Gyeongsangbuk-do Provincial Orchestra
Invited performances at Berlin Cathedral, Mikhailovsky Castle in Saint Petersburg, the Chopin University of Music, and La Folle Journée Warsaw
Served as adjunct professor at Chonnam National University, Keimyung University, Changwon National University, Yewon School, Seoul Arts High School, and Daegu Catholic University
Leader of Ensemble Maru and Ensemble Tara, Music Director of Ensemble Tonica, and Principal of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber
Currently: Teaches at Hanyang University, Kyungpook National University, Keimyung University, and Gyeongbuk Arts High School

kim-sojeong.name: Kim So-jeong
bio: Completed the Advanced Soloist program at Freiburg University of Music, Germany
Completed the Soloist program at the Strasbourg Conservatory, France
Completed the Ph.D. coursework in Musicology at Hanyang University

Performed as soloist with numerous orchestras including the Yalta Symphony, the Zaporozhye Philharmonic, the Daegu Symphony Orchestra, the Gyeongsangbuk-do Provincial Orchestra, the Pohang Philharmonic Orchestra, the Korean National Police Symphony Orchestra, the Hwaseong City Symphony Orchestra, and the Daegu MBC Symphony Orchestra
Invited recitals at Schloss Bürgeln, the Freiburg Augustinum, and Badenweiler in Germany, the Strasbourg Young Artists series in France, and the Samsun Opera House in Turkey
Currently: Teaches at Daegu Catholic University, Daegu National University of Education, and Gyeongbuk Arts High School; Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, the Modern Ensemble, and the Emanon Ensemble

kim-eunji.name: Kim Eun-ji
bio: Graduated from the College of Arts at Kyungpook National University
Master's degree from the Hanover University of Music, Drama and Media, Germany
Graduated with highest honors from the Konzertexamen (Soloist) program at Mainz University of Music, Germany
Served as a non-permanent member of the Heidelberg Theater and the Hessen State Theater in Germany
Performed as soloist with the Daegu Symphony Orchestra, the Beethoven Orchestra Hessen, and the Mainzer Musici
Invited performances at the Westfalen MLW Philharmonie and the Mainz State Museum in Germany, the Chopin University of Music, and La Folle Journée Warsaw
Selected artist in the music category of the 5th Daegu Foundation for Culture Young Artist program
1st prize at the Korea-Germany Brahms Society Competition
Served as a lecturer at Kyungpook National University
Currently: Teaches at the Daegu Institute for the Gifted in Arts and Gyeongbuk Arts High School; Member of Trio Beatrice, Ensemble Blanche, and Ensemble Maru, and Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber

lee-eunjeong.name: Lee Eun-jeong
bio: Admitted as a gifted student to Korea National University of Arts while attending Seoul Arts High School (graduated with Bachelor's and Master's degrees)
Master's degree from Indiana University and Doctorate from Boston University

1st prize at the Monthly Music Competition, 1st prize at the TBC Competition, Grand Prize at the Gyeongbuk Arts High School Competition, and 3rd prizes at the Nanpa Competition and the Hankook Ilbo Competition
Performed as soloist with the Daegu Symphony Orchestra, the Daejeon Philharmonic Orchestra, and the Haffner Sinfonietta Chamber Orchestra (Boston)
Served as a lecturer at Korea National University of Arts and the Korea National Institute for the Gifted in Arts
Currently: Teaches at Kyungpook National University, Keimyung University, and Gyeongbuk Arts High School; Principal of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber and Member of Ensemble Dongsung

baek-nahyun.name: Baek Na-hyun
bio: Graduated from the Department of Music at the College of Arts, Kyungpook National University
Graduated with highest honors from the Hanover University of Music, Drama and Media, Germany
Numerous invited performances and recitals at home and abroad, centered on Europe, including the Mosel Music Festival, the Mozart Music Festival, and the Trzęsacz International Music Festival
Performed as soloist with numerous orchestras including the Hanover Chamber Orchestra in Germany and the Gyeongsangbuk-do Provincial Orchestra
Served as Principal of the Hanover Chamber Orchestra in Germany and as Guest Concertmaster of numerous orchestras including the Daegu Gala Orchestra

Currently: Teaches at Kyungpook National University, Gyeongbuk Arts High School, Gimcheon Arts High School, and the Daegu Institute for the Gifted in Arts; Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, Concertmaster of the Novo Philharmonic Orchestra, and Leader of the Picasso Ensemble

kim-suji.name: Kim Su-ji
bio: Bachelor's and Master's degrees from the Hochschule für Musik Saar, Germany
Artist Diploma and Doctorate from the University of Cincinnati, College-Conservatory of Music, USA
Released the album 'Saint-Saëns Violin Concertos No.3 & No.2' on the SONY Classical Label

Currently: Concertmaster of the Daegu International Broadcasting Symphony Orchestra; Member of the Daegu Contemporary Music Festival (DCMF) Ensemble, Ensemble DNCE, and Daegu Virtuoso Chamber; teaches at Daegu Catholic University and its graduate school, the Daegu Institute for the Gifted in Arts, and Gyeongbuk Arts High School

han-hyemin.name: Han Hye-min
bio: Graduated with top honors from Daegu Catholic University
Completed the Advanced Soloist program at Detmold University of Music, Germany
Served as a Solist member of the Detmold Barock Orchester in Germany
Performed as soloist with numerous orchestras including the Debrecen Orchestra in Hungary, the Daegu Phil Orchestra, the Daegu Catholic University Orchestra, and the Daegu Music Festival Orchestra
Currently: Member of the DNCE Ensemble, Concertmaster of the Korea Sori Chamber Orchestra, teaches at Gyeongbuk Arts High School, and Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber

bae-eunjin.name: Bae Eun-jin
bio: Graduated from Korea National University of Arts
Completed the GPD at the Peabody Institute of Johns Hopkins University
Awarded the Peabody Singapore Fellowship Award
Selected as the 2020 Suseong Artpia Artist in Muhak

Currently: Principal of the Daegu International Broadcasting Symphony Orchestra, Guest Principal of the Dio Orchestra, Principal of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, Member of the Busan Chamber Society, and Member of Ensemble Dongsung and DNCE

park-soyeon.name: Park So-yeon
bio: Graduated from the Department of Music at the College of Arts and its graduate school, Kyungpook National University
Completed the Advanced Soloist program at the Academy of Performing Arts in Prague (HAMU)
Served as a guest member of the Hradec Králové Philharmonic Orchestra in Prague
Resident musician at the Biwon Music Hall

Currently: Lecturer at the Lifelong Education Center of Daegu National University of Education and the Andong Youth Orchestra; Member of Ensemble Maru, Emanon, and the Modern Ensemble; and active as a member and professional performer of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber

lee-jeongmin.name: Lee Jeong-min
bio: Master's degree from the Department of Orchestral Instruments at the College of Music and Performing Arts and its graduate school, Keimyung University
Vordiplom from HfM Detmold, Germany
Diploma from the Accademia Internazionale di Musica in Roma
Diploma from the Spain Festival Academia de Musica International Tenerife
Currently: Representative of Ensemble BOAZ and Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber

oh-kookhwan.name: Oh Kook-hwan
bio: Diplom from Rostock University of Music and Theatre, Germany
Master's and Soloist programs from Detmold University of Music, Germany
Completed the conducting program at the Hamburg Brahms Conservatory, Germany

Currently: Teaches at Daegu Catholic University, Gyeongbuk Arts High School, and the Daegu Institute for the Gifted in Arts; Conductor of the Suseong Pops Orchestra, the Maruhan Youth Orchestra, and the Gunwi Middle School 'Neo' Orchestra
Principal Cellist of the CM Symphony Orchestra and the Maruhan Philharmonic Orchestra, Music Director of the original creative group 'Sorigyeol', and Director of the SoundT Ensemble
Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber, the Soloist Cello Ensemble Gyeongsang, Ensemble BOAZ, and the Canticum Novum Mission Choir

lee-heesoo.name: Lee Hee-soo
bio: Bachelor of Music from Korea National University of Arts
Master's degree from Dresden University of Music, Germany

Won prizes at numerous competitions including the Daegu Music Association, the Masan Music Association, the Daejeon Music Association, the Music Education Newspaper, the Seongjeong Music Competition, the Music Journal, and the Eumak Chunchu competitions

Guest performer with the Ditto Orchestra, the Cheonan Philharmonic Orchestra, and the Gyeongsangbuk-do Provincial Orchestra, and Guest Principal of the Stadt Philharmonic, the Dio Orchestra, and the Grand Symphony Orchestra

Currently: Member of Ensemble BOAZ, Ensemble Sol, Ensemble Neustadt, the Busan Virtuoso Ensemble, the Soloist Cello Ensemble Gyeongsang, The Cellist of KNUA, Ensemble Sonore, and Trio Bellino; Member of Daegu Virtuoso Chamber; Principal Cellist of the Gyeongsan Philharmonic Orchestra; and teaches at Pohang Arts High School

kim-minji.name: Kim Min-ji
bio: Won the Astral Artists National Auditions in the United States in 2003 and made her American stage debut
Admitted as a gifted student to Korea National University of Arts at age 16 and graduated
Master's degree at the New England Conservatory in the United States, graduating from the Professional Studies and Soloist programs on full scholarship
Selected as a recipient of the Frank Huntington Beebe Fund in 2005 and completed the Soloist program at the Toulouse Conservatory in France
1st prizes at the Dong-A Ilbo, JoongAng Ilbo, Hankook Ilbo, and Chosun Ilbo competitions, and Grand Prizes at the Nanpa Competition and the KBS New Artist Music Competition

First Korean recipient of a scholarship at the Emanuel Feuermann International Cello Competition; 1st prizes at the Hudson Valley String Competition, the New England Conservatory String Competition, and the HAMS International Cello Competition; and 1st prize plus a special award for the commissioned work at the Irving M. Klein International String Competition

Performed as soloist with the KBS Symphony Orchestra, the Seoul Philharmonic Orchestra, the Suwon Philharmonic Orchestra, the Wonju Philharmonic Orchestra, the Incheon Philharmonic Orchestra, the Gunsan Philharmonic Orchestra, the Chungnam Provincial Orchestra, the Daegu MBC Symphony Orchestra, and the Gangnam Symphony Orchestra

Served as Associate Principal of the Reina Sofía Orchestra in Spain led by Lorin Maazel (the first Asian to do so), Principal of the Seoul Arts Center Festival Orchestra, and Professor at Keimyung University

Currently: Professor in the Department of Instrumental Music at the College of Music, Seoul National University; Music Director of the Deoksugung Seokjojeon Concert; and Principal of the Cellista Cello Ensemble
